executiveSummary.oneLiner: A task management SaaS API with real-time updates, RBAC, and REST/WebSocket interfaces
objectives: Provide a reliable REST API for task and project management with RBAC
Enable real-time updates via WebSocket with project channel subscriptions
Deliver a fully tested, containerized, CI-ready deployment package
targetAudience: Development teams needing structured task tracking
SaaS operators deploying multi-tenant task tools

inScope: REST API for users, projects, tasks, and project members
JWT-based authentication with refresh token rotation
Three-tier RBAC: admin, member, viewer roles
WebSocket server for real-time task and project change events
PostgreSQL schema with migrations and seed data
Docker and GitHub Actions CI/CD configuration
outOfScope: GraphQL interface is out of scope for the MVP release
OAuth or SSO integration deferred to post-MVP roadmap
Mobile client applications are not in scope for this release
Redis or other caching layers are excluded from the MVP design

projectOverview.background: Teams working on software projects need a reliable, structured way to track tasks, assign responsibilities, and coordinate work. Existing tools are either too complex or lack real-time collaboration features that modern teams expect.
projectOverview.problemStatement: Development teams lack a lightweight, API-first task management solution with fine-grained RBAC and real-time event streaming that integrates cleanly with their existing toolchains.
projectOverview.proposedSolution: Build a REST API backed by PostgreSQL with JWT authentication, three-tier RBAC, and WebSocket-based real-time event broadcasting. The API-first approach enables integration with any frontend or third-party tooling.
successCriteria: All CRUD endpoints respond within 100ms at P50 under 100 concurrent users
WebSocket events are delivered within 200ms of database state changes
100% of RBAC rules are enforced with no privilege escalation vulnerabilities
Integration test suite achieves 80% code coverage with CI/CD passing on all PRs

assumptions.description: PostgreSQL 16.x is available in the deployment environment
Node.js 20.x LTS is the target runtime environment
All clients will handle JWT token refresh before expiry

technicalArchitecture.overview: TaskFlow uses a layered architecture: Express HTTP layer → middleware (auth, RBAC, logging, errors) → route handlers → Kysely data access layer → PostgreSQL. WebSocket connections share the JWT auth layer and broadcast events through a project channel subscription model.

diagrams.title: TaskFlow System Architecture
diagrams.source:
flowchart TD
  Client[Client] --> REST[REST API]
  Client --> WS[WebSocket Server]
  REST --> Auth[Auth Middleware]
  WS --> Auth
  Auth --> RBAC[RBAC Middleware]
  RBAC --> Routes[Route Handlers]
  Routes --> Kysely[Kysely Query Builder]
  Kysely --> PG[(PostgreSQL 16)]
  Routes --> Broadcast[Event Broadcaster]
  Broadcast --> WS

diagrams.title: JWT Authentication Flow
diagrams.source:
sequenceDiagram
  participant C as Client
  participant API as Express API
  participant DB as PostgreSQL
  C->>API: POST /auth/login {email, password}
  API->>DB: SELECT user WHERE email=?
  DB-->>API: user record
  API->>API: bcrypt.compare(password, hash)
  API-->>C: {accessToken, refreshToken}
  C->>API: GET /projects (Authorization: Bearer <token>)
  API->>API: jwt.verify(token)
  API-->>C: 200 OK {projects}

patterns.name: Repository Pattern
patterns.rationale: Separates data access logic from business logic, enabling unit testing of route handlers without database dependencies

patterns.name: Middleware Chain
patterns.rationale: Express middleware pipeline provides composable, reusable cross-cutting concerns such as auth, RBAC, logging, and error handling

patterns.name: Event Broadcasting
patterns.rationale: Project channel subscriptions enable selective event delivery without broadcasting all changes to all connected clients

components.name: AuthMiddleware
components.description: Extracts and verifies JWT tokens from Authorization headers, attaches user context to requests
responsibilities: Parse Bearer token from Authorization header
Verify JWT signature and expiry with HS256
Attach decoded user payload to request context
Return 401 for missing or invalid tokens

components.name: RBACMiddleware
components.description: Enforces role-based access control by checking project membership and role hierarchy
responsibilities: Query project_members table for user role in project
Enforce three-tier role hierarchy: admin > member > viewer
Return 404 for non-members to prevent project enumeration
Allow global admin role to bypass project-level checks

components.name: WebSocketServer
components.description: Manages WebSocket connections with JWT auth on upgrade, project channel subscriptions, and heartbeat
responsibilities: Authenticate WebSocket upgrade requests via JWT query parameter
Manage project channel subscriptions with subscribe/unsubscribe messages
Broadcast task and project change events to subscribed clients
Maintain heartbeat ping/pong every 30s with 2-missed disconnect

dataModel.overview: TaskFlow uses four normalized PostgreSQL tables: users, projects, tasks, and project_members. All primary keys use UUIDs generated by gen_random_uuid(). All timestamps use TIMESTAMPTZ. Status and role fields use VARCHAR(20) for extensibility.

entities.name: users
entities.description: Stores user accounts with hashed passwords and global role

entities.name: projects
entities.description: Stores project metadata with soft-delete support

apiDesign.overview: REST API following JSON conventions with standard HTTP methods. All endpoints return JSON. Authentication uses Bearer JWT tokens. Errors use a consistent {error, message, details} format.

endpoints.description: Authenticate user and return token pair
Rotate refresh token and return new access token
List tasks with filtering and pagination

apiDesign.authStrategy: JWT Bearer tokens. Access tokens expire in 15 minutes. Refresh tokens expire in 7 days and rotate on each use.

securityConsiderations.overview: Security follows OWASP Top 10 guidelines. JWT HS256 with configurable secrets. Bcrypt with 12 salt rounds. RBAC enforced at middleware layer. Non-member requests return 404 to prevent enumeration.

threatModel.threat: JWT token theft via XSS or network interception
threatModel.mitigation: Short access token lifetime (15min), refresh token rotation with theft detection

threatModel.threat: Project enumeration by non-members via error messages
threatModel.mitigation: Return 404 Not Found (not 403 Forbidden) for non-member access to projects

threatModel.threat: Brute force attacks on authentication endpoints
threatModel.mitigation: Rate limiting on auth endpoints, bcrypt cost factor 12, account lockout after failures

testingStrategy.overview: Testing follows the pyramid with integration tests at the top. Vitest with supertest for API tests. Real PostgreSQL test database with isolated schema per suite.

levels.name: integration
levels.description: Full API integration tests using supertest against real PostgreSQL. Each suite creates an isolated test schema and tears down after all tests complete.

levels.name: unit
levels.description: Unit tests for utility functions, validators, and middleware logic in isolation.

deploymentPlan.overview: Deployed via Docker containers on any OCI-compliant runtime. GitHub Actions CI pipeline runs lint, test, and build on every push and pull request.

environments.name: ci
environments.infrastructure: GitHub Actions runners with PostgreSQL service container

deploymentPlan.cicdPipeline: GitHub Actions: lint → unit tests → integration tests → Docker build → push to registry

risks.description: Database migration complexity increases over time as schema evolves with new features
risks.mitigation: Use versioned SQL migration files with explicit rollback scripts and test in CI

risks.description: JWT secret rotation requires all active sessions to be invalidated simultaneously
risks.mitigation: Implement refresh token blacklist table and document rotation procedure

risks.description: WebSocket connection scaling may become a bottleneck under high concurrent user load
risks.mitigation: Design stateless WebSocket handlers that can be scaled horizontally with sticky sessions

phases.name: Phase 1: Foundation
phases.description: Database schema, API contracts, auth strategy, WebSocket design, and architecture decisions

phases.name: Phase 2: Implementation
phases.description: Database migrations, auth middleware, CRUD routes, and WebSocket server

diagram.title: TaskFlow Execution Timeline
diagram.source:
gantt
  title TaskFlow Project Timeline
  dateFormat  YYYY-MM-DD
  section Phase 1
  Database Schema     :TASK-001, 2026-02-25, 1d
  API Contracts       :TASK-002, after TASK-001, 1d
  Auth Strategy       :TASK-003, after TASK-001, 1d
  WebSocket Design    :TASK-004, after TASK-001, 1d
  ADR Debate          :TASK-005, after TASK-002, 1d
  section Phase 2
  DB Migrations       :TASK-006, after TASK-005, 1d
  Auth Middleware     :TASK-007, after TASK-006, 1d
  User Routes         :TASK-008, after TASK-007, 1d
  Project/Task Routes :TASK-009, after TASK-007, 1d
  WebSocket Server    :TASK-010, after TASK-007, 1d
  section Phase 3
  Integration Tests   :TASK-011, after TASK-010, 2d
  Deployment Config   :TASK-012, after TASK-011, 1d

terms.definition: Role-Based Access Control — permission model based on user roles within a project
JSON Web Token — compact, URL-safe means of representing claims for authentication
Type-safe TypeScript SQL query builder used instead of an ORM per ADR-001
PostgreSQL timestamp with timezone — all timestamps stored with UTC offset
WebSocket ping/pong mechanism to detect and disconnect stale connections

adrs.title: SQL query builder (Kysely) over ORM for data access
adrs.summary: After debate comparing Kysely, Prisma, and Drizzle, the team chose Kysely for its type safety without ORM magic, aligning with the no-ORM constraint.

references.title: Kysely Documentation
references.description: Type-safe SQL query builder for TypeScript

references.title: OWASP Top 10
references.description: Security risk reference used in threat model
